title: Youth-Led Climate Action for Resilient Communities: “SAWN” (Preservation) Project
content: Generations For Peace, with UNICEF and national partners, empowers youth across Jordan through SAWN to lead climate action and resilience efforts. Since 2022, youth have mobilised thousands of volunteers and initiatives; in 2026–2027 SAWN will scale leadership, community impact, and national climate advocacy.

title: Generations For Peace
content: Generations For Peace (GFP) is a Jordan-based organisation dedicated to advancing youth-led peacebuilding, social cohesion, and community resilience.
snippet: Generations For Peace (GFP) is a Jordan-based organisation dedicated to advancing youth-led peacebuilding, social cohesion, and community resilience.

title: Water at the Heart of Climate Action
content: Water at the Heart of Climate Action (WHCA) tackles one of the most urgent challenges of our time: the growing impact of climate change on water ecosystems and the people who depend on them.
snippet: Water at the Heart of Climate Action (WHCA) tackles one of the most urgent challenges of our time: the growing impact of climate change on water ecosystems and the people who depend on them.

title: Resilience, real and lived: Lessons on strengthening Caribbean MSMEs
content: Work on micro, small and medium-sized enterprise (MSME) resilience often centres on frameworks, tools and systems. Yet resilience is ultimately about people and the realities businesses face every day.
snippet: Work on micro, small and medium-sized enterprise (MSME) resilience often centres on frameworks, tools and systems. Yet resilience is ultimately about people and the realities businesses face every day

title: Weather shocks and unintended fertility in Sub-Saharan Africa
content: This paper examines the effects of drought shocks on unintended pregnancies across 18 countries in Sub-Saharan Africa.
snippet: This paper examines the effects of drought shocks on unintended pregnancies across 18 countries in Sub-Saharan Africa.

title: CHIM intervention guidance: Low carbon climate resilient waste management
content: This guide shows how low- and middle-income countries (LMICs) can integrate low carbon and climate resilient principles into health care waste management in a phased, practical way.
snippet: This guide shows how low- and middle-income countries (LMICs) can integrate low carbon and climate resilient principles into health care waste management in a phased, practical way.

title: Gender Equality and Social Inclusion in National Adaptation Plan Processes
content: The NAP Global Network’s self-paced online course will help you strengthen your own work by unpacking how gender equality and social inclusion (GESI) components are integrated in the NAP process.
snippet: The NAP Global Network’s self-paced online course will help you strengthen your own work by unpacking how gender equality and social inclusion (GESI) components are integrated in the NAP process.

title: IAG renews Aboriginal Carbon Foundation partnership to help strengthen community resilience as bushfire risk increase
content: General insurer IAG has renewed its partnership with the Aboriginal Carbon Foundation (AbCF), supporting AbCF on its mission to increase awareness and adoption of cultural land management to strengthen community resilience in a changing climate.

title: Assessment of multiple predictors to the psychological effects of flooding for residential and business sectors in Peninsular Malaysia
content: A new emerging research was conducted to understand the severity of psychological impacts of flooding in flood-prone regions in Peninsular Malaysia.
snippet: A new emerging research was conducted to understand the severity of psychological impacts of flooding in flood-prone regions in Peninsular Malaysia.

title: Digital risks: New report maps critical vulnerabilities in the world’s interconnected systems
content: Digital technologies have revolutionized how we live, connect, and work, yet our growing reliance on these systems has created risks that often go unnoticed.
snippet: Digital technologies have revolutionized how we live, connect, and work, yet our growing reliance on these systems has created risks that often go unnoticed.

title: When digital systems fail: An expert report on the hidden risks of our digital world
content: This expert report examines how digital infrastructure, now central to essential services such as healthcare, finance, and emergency response, is creating new forms of systemic risk through increasing interdependence.
snippet: This expert report examines how digital infrastructure, now central to essential services such as healthcare, finance, and emergency response, is creating new forms of systemic risk through increasing

title: Stories of resilience: lessons from local adaptation practice
content: This report brings together a set of grounded, practice-based insights on how adaptation can be more effective when it is led from the local level.
snippet: This report brings together a set of grounded, practice-based insights on how adaptation can be more effective when it is led from the local level.

title: From forecasts to futures: how Ugandan communities are turning early warnings into everyday action
content: The Uganda Red Cross Society identified the most vulnerable and underserved communities and layered with catchment management plans from the Ministry of Water and Environment.
snippet: The Uganda Red Cross Society identified the most vulnerable and underserved communities and layered with catchment management plans from the Ministry of Water and Environment.

title: Ten charts to discover the European State of the Climate 2025 report
content: The European State of the Climate (ESOTC) 2025 offers an overview of the climate during the previous year through a detailed analysis of key climate variables.
snippet: The European State of the Climate (ESOTC) 2025 offers an overview of the climate during the previous year through a detailed analysis of key climate variables.

title: Better preparedness and warning systems needed to deal with ice-rock avalanches
content: This study compares the Chamoli disaster with the 2025 Blatten avalanche in Switzerland, where early warning, and prompt communication and response, worked to evacuate people to safety.
snippet: This study compares the Chamoli disaster with the 2025 Blatten avalanche in Switzerland, where early warning, and prompt communication and response, worked to evacuate people to safety.

title: Planetary solvency: tipping into the wild unknown
content: The report from the Institute and Faculty of Actuaries (IFoA) and ARU warns that biodiversity loss, climate shocks and geopolitical conflict are disrupting the food system, risking catastrophic impacts for the financial system and for society as a whole.
snippet: The report from the Institute and Faculty of Actuaries (IFoA) and ARU warns that biodiversity loss, climate shocks and geopolitical conflict are disrupting the food system, risking catastrophic impact

title: How wildfires worsen flood risk
content: A new approach to analyzing watersheds shows how storms occurring after a wildfire can have higher flooding risk than similar storms that occurred before a fire.
snippet: A new approach to analyzing watersheds shows how storms occurring after a wildfire can have higher flooding risk than similar storms that occurred before a fire.

title: Traditional models still ‘outperform AI’ for extreme weather forecasts
content: Computer models that use artificial intelligence (AI) cannot forecast record-breaking weather as well as traditional climate models, according to a new study.
snippet: Computer models that use artificial intelligence (AI) cannot forecast record-breaking weather as well as traditional climate models, according to a new study.

title: Empowering Fijian villages through nature-based coastal protection
content: Green-gray infrastructure helps confront rising seas for a more resilient future for Fijian communities.
snippet: Green-gray infrastructure helps confront rising seas for a more resilient future for Fijian communities.

title: The climate crisis explained in seven graphs
content: There are hopes that as nations improve energy efficiency and move away from fossil fuels, emissions could drop even more.
snippet: There are hopes that as nations improve energy efficiency and move away from fossil fuels, emissions could drop even more.

title: Inclusive heritage for disaster risk reduction: Digital and participatory methods for resilient communities
content: Participants will gain hands-on experience with digital & participatory methods, working directly with communities to design inclusive projects, assess local needs & apply strategies that integrate heritage, disaster risk management and climate adaptation

title: Adaptation insights – Climate-resilient health systems for sustained value chains, healthcare access and services
content: This edition of the adaptation insights series addresses the specific question of how health systems can be made climate resilient in order to sustain value chains, healthcare services, and access to healthcare.

title: Snow cover on Greek mountains has more than halved in four decades, study finds
content: Snow cover in the mountains of Greece – an important water source for communities, agriculture and natural ecosystems during the dry summer months – has more than halved over the past four decades, a study has found.

title: Study shows why rainfall remains hard to predict in a warming world
content: A study led by the University of Oxford and ETH Zurich reveals that a key part of the climate system can be underestimated by current climate models, helping explain why forecasts of regional rainfall remain uncertain.
snippet: A study led by the University of Oxford and ETH Zurich reveals that a key part of the climate system can be underestimated by current climate models, helping explain why forecasts of regional rainfall

title: Urban areas host 80% of England’s homes at high risk of flooding, study finds
content: Exclusive: 839,000 homes in urban areas face threat of surface-water flooding, with social housing tenants most vulnerable to costs.
snippet: Exclusive: 839,000 homes in urban areas face threat of surface-water flooding, with social housing tenants most vulnerable to costs.

title: A geoAI-driven and decision-oriented methodology for multi-hazard early warning system development
content: Drawing on ethnographic fieldwork in Japan (and partially Chile) since 2016, this article argues that disaster preparedness education is increasingly delivered through playful, fun activities.
snippet: Drawing on ethnographic fieldwork in Japan (and partially Chile) since 2016, this article argues that disaster preparedness education is increasingly delivered through playful, fun activities.

title: Flood first response hackathon: multiscale sensing and robotics for early warning and emergency response
content: After this hackaton, the students can implement new robotic systems in early warning systems. They will be able to correctly choose the components for their idea based on their specifications, test their compatibility and efficiently implement them.

title: Playful approaches to disaster preparedness with Chika Watanabe
content: Join the UCL Risk & Disaster Reduction monthly event celebrating UCL’s Bicentenary, featuring a talk by Dr Chika Watanabe on playful approaches to disaster preparedness.
snippet: Join the UCL Risk & Disaster Reduction monthly event celebrating UCL’s Bicentenary, featuring a talk by Dr Chika Watanabe on playful approaches to disaster preparedness.

title: From policy to practice: Burkina Faso strengthens early warning systems and climate resilience
content: Burkina Faso’s experience illustrates how coordinated investments in infrastructure, systems, and capacity development can generate tangible improvements in early warning services and disaster risk reduction.

title: Climate adaptation investment planning: Lessons learned
content: This report explores how developing countries can convert their climate adaptation goals into investment-ready programs that incorporate public and private financing, generate strong economic returns, and help drive development benefits.

title: Disaster Risk Reduction Summer School
content: Join leading DRR experts for a five‑day summer school exploring why disasters are not natural, how human decisions drive risk, and what effective, accountable disaster reduction looks like.
snippet: Join leading DRR experts for a five‑day summer school exploring why disasters are not natural, how human decisions drive risk, and what effective, accountable disaster reduction looks like.

title: AI enhancing efficiency and preparedness in disaster supply chains worldwide
content: The use of artificial intelligence (AI) tools in disaster supply chains has expanded sharply in recent years, with new research identifying it as a key driver of resilience in the face of rising global disruptions.

title: Research uses AI to unlock decades of hidden flood risk data
content: University of Houston engineers reveal how Houston’s flood risk has shifted over time.
snippet: University of Houston engineers reveal how Houston’s flood risk has shifted over time.

title: Study: Faced with a hotter future, America needs better data and response plans
content: A University of Kansas study finds poor data and unclear agency responsibilities leave the U.S. unprepared for extreme heat.
snippet: A University of Kansas study finds poor data and unclear agency responsibilities leave the U.S. unprepared for extreme heat.

title: Nationwide tool helps answer do we have enough water
content: The U.S. Geological Survey released the first nationwide tool to help water planners determine if communities have enough water for current and future needs.
snippet: The U.S. Geological Survey released the first nationwide tool to help water planners determine if communities have enough water for current and future needs.

title: Securing tenure and building resilience: lessons from Harare
content: This study, which describes how residents in three of the city’s informal settlements are responding to climate-related hazards, shows how tenure security can improve resilience through community-led settlement upgrading.
